beats.activity: Wake up, tend sourdough starter, coffee on the porch with a handwritten letter.; Shower, breakfast, prepare for a hike.
beats.reasoning: Sunday morning ritual—Raj starts every day this way, especially after a low-energy Saturday. The quiet porch time anchors his routine and sets the tone for a reflective day.

beats.activity: Drive to Discovery Park for a morning hike.
beats.reasoning: Discovery Park is minutes from home and perfect for a Sunday morning walk; the sunny 72F weather is ideal. No traffic on a Sunday morning means a short, scenic drive.

beats.activity: Hike the trails at Discovery Park, enjoying the views and quiet.
beats.reasoning: A 2+ hour hike is Raj's typical Sunday activity when weather cooperates. Low energy from yesterday makes this a restorative, solitary outdoor reset.
Back to Magnolia for lunch prep; short local drive, allowing time to cool off after the hike.

beats.activity: Prepare and enjoy a leisurely lunch—fresh greens, bread, maybe leftover sourdough.
beats.reasoning: After hiking, Raj takes time to eat mindfully at home, tending his starter and settling into the afternoon rhythm.

beats.activity: Volunteer at local food bank, stocking shelves and helping organize donations.
beats.reasoning: Sunday afternoons are when Raj often volunteers; it's part of his routine and aligns with his values. Low energy from yesterday doesn't stop him from showing up for his community.

beats.activity: Stop at Whole Foods to pick up ingredients for dinner—fresh fish, vegetables, and any sourdough supplies needed.
beats.reasoning: On the way home from volunteering, a quick errand for dinner prep. Sunday shopping is part of his weekly rhythm.
Short walk or drive back to Magnolia from Whole Foods; settling in for the evening.

beats.activity: Tend the sourdough starter, bake a fresh loaf, and prepare dinner on the back porch.
beats.reasoning: Sunday evening is Raj's sacred time—baking, cooking, and tending his starter. The calm sunny weather means he can spend time on the porch, grounding himself for the week ahead.

beats.activity: Eat dinner slowly, enjoying the evening light and the view from the porch.
beats.reasoning: Mindful dinner aligns with his routine; the golden hour on a Sunday is when Raj feels most present.

beats.activity: Write handwritten letters, listen to music, and wind down for the evening.
beats.reasoning: Final wind-down ritual—handwriting letters is how Raj processes the week and prepares mentally for tax season ahead. Quiet, intentional, grounding.

world_event_prompt: Calm, sunny Sunday, high of 72F. Quiet end-of-week day with no major disruptions. Lake Union sees heavy recreational boat and paddle traffic in the afternoon.
